## Section 1: Project Structure

**Files found:**
- `Dockerfile` — container build definition
- `package.json` — Node.js manifest
- `docs/security/` — pipeline artifacts only

**Note:** `server.js` referenced in `Dockerfile CMD` is absent from the repository. This is a minimal E2E test fixture with no application source code.

## Section 2: Dependencies

| Package | Version | Known Issues |
|---|---|---|
| `express` | 4.19.2 | No critical CVEs at assessment date |
| `lodash` | 4.17.10 | CVE-2019-10744 (High, prototype pollution), CVE-2020-8203 (High, prototype pollution) |
| `sequelize` | 6.37.1 | No critical CVEs at assessment date; raw query capability warrants scrutiny |

**Supply chain gaps:**
- No `package-lock.json` or `yarn.lock` — dependency pinning absent (SC-002 violation)
- No `.npmrc` — no registry pinning

## Section 3: Authentication & Authorization

- No authentication middleware detected in source
- No JWT, session, or OAuth libraries declared
- No `passport`, `jsonwebtoken`, `express-jwt` in dependencies
- **Assessment**: Authentication entirely absent from declared dependencies

## Section 4: Secrets & Credentials

- No hardcoded secrets found in scanned files
- No `.env` files present
- No `secrets/`, `credentials/`, or similar directories

## Section 5: Cryptography

- No crypto usage detected in source (no source code)
- No `bcrypt`, `argon2`, or similar password-hashing libraries in dependencies

## Section 6: Input Validation & Injection

- No input validation libraries (`joi`, `express-validator`, `zod`) in dependencies
- `sequelize@6.37.1` present — SQL injection risk depends on usage of raw queries
- `lodash@4.17.10` — prototype pollution via `_.merge()`, `_.set()`, `_.zipObjectDeep()` [CVE-2019-10744]

## Section 7: Security Controls

| Control | Status | Evidence |
|---|---|---|
| Security headers (helmet) | 🔴 Missing | Not in dependencies |
| CORS configuration | 🔴 Missing | No `cors` package |

| Logging | 🔴 Missing | No logging library (morgan, winston, pino) |
| TLS/HTTPS | 🔴 Missing | Not configured in Dockerfile |

## Section 8: Infrastructure / IaC

**Dockerfile findings:**
- `FROM node:20-alpine` — mutable tag, no digest pinning (IF-007 violation)
- No `USER` directive — container runs as root (IF-002 VIOLATION — critical)
- No `HEALTHCHECK`
- No read-only filesystem configuration (IF-003 gap)
- `RUN npm install --production` — no `--ignore-scripts` flag (supply chain risk)

## Section 9: Routes

No routes found. Route inventory pre-pass returned 0 routes (`.route-inventory.json`).

## Section 10: Logging & Monitoring

- No structured logging library detected
- No security event logging
- No SIEM/aggregation configuration

## Section 11: Container / Deployment

- Single Docker container on port 3000
- No Kubernetes manifests, no docker-compose
- No reverse proxy configuration
- No TLS termination

## Section 12: Testing & CI/CD

- No CI/CD configuration (no `.github/workflows/`, no `.gitlab-ci.yml`)
- No test files found

## Summary

This is a minimal fixture repository with 3 declared npm dependencies and a Dockerfile. No application source code is present beyond the entrypoint reference (`server.js`). The primary security signals are:

1. `lodash@4.17.10` — known prototype pollution vulnerabilities (High severity)
2. Dockerfile runs as root — container privilege escalation risk (Critical)
3. No lockfile — supply chain integrity gap
4. No security middleware stack — auth, CORS, rate-limiting, headers all absent
5. No input validation — injection attack surface unknown (no source)
6. Dockerfile uses mutable base image tag — reproducibility gap
